title: Unit 1: Getting Started Exercises
description: Apply basic logic, math, recursion, and string manipulation on beginner exercises from the laboratory manual.

topics.title: 1.1 Sum of Last Digits
topics.description: Extract the last digit of two integers (ignoring their sign) using the modulo operator (%) and return their sum.
topics.cheatSheet:
// Extract last digit
int lastDigit = Math.abs(num) % 10;

ispo.desc: Accepts two integers input1 and input2 to process.
Allocates memory for digit1, digit2, and result variables to store units and computed sum.
Applies Math.abs() to discard negative signs, computes modulo 10 to extract units digit, and adds them.
Returns the integer sum back to the caller.

quiz.q: Why is Math.abs() necessary when extracting digits using modulo 10?
o: Because Java modulo preserves the negative sign of the dividend (e.g. -267 % 10 = -7)

topics.title: 1.2 Is N an exact multiple of M?
topics.description: Determine if integer N is an exact multiple of M. Return 2 if true, 1 if false, and 3 if either parameter is 0.
topics.cheatSheet:
if (num1 == 0 || num2 == 0) return 3;
if (num1 % num2 == 0) return 2; else return 1;

ispo.desc: Takes the two integer parameters.
Checks if either input is zero, then checks divisibility using the modulo (%) operator.
Returns 3 if either parameter is 0.
Returns 2 if num1 is an exact multiple of num2.
Returns 1 if num1 is not an exact multiple.

quiz.q: What does the function return if num1 = 12 and num2 = 3?

topics.title: 1.3 Combine Strings
topics.description: Given 2 strings of different lengths, return a combined string in the format: short+long+short.
topics.cheatSheet:
int l1 = a.length(), l2 = b.length();
String result = (l1 < l2) ? (a + b + a) : (b + a + b);

ispo.desc: Receives the two string inputs.
Declares and stores l1 and l2 representing string lengths.
Declares result variable to store the final combined string.
Compares length storage variables and structures the concatenated string format (short+long+short).
Returns the combined string.

quiz.q: What is the output of combine("hi", "hello")?

topics.title: 1.4 Even or Odd
topics.description: Given 5 integers and a type string ('even' or 'odd'), return the count of how many parameters match the requested type.

ispo.desc: Accepts 5 integer parameters and a filter type string.
Structures integers into numbers array, and allocates count variable to track matches.
Creates loop check boolean variable isEven to hold temporary parity.
Loops through elements, checks absolute parity and matches against type string, updating count.
Returns matched items count.

topics.title: 1.5 Second Last Digit of a Number
topics.description: Extract the digit in the tens place of a given number. Return -1 if it doesn't exist (single-digit). Always return a positive value.

ispo.desc: Accepts integer 'num'.
Stores the absolute value of the number to ignore negative signs.
Stores the extracted tens digit in result variable.
Checks if number is single digit (no tens place).
Divides by 10 to shift units out, and takes modulo 10 to extract the second last digit.
Returns -1 for single digit boundary case.
Returns tens digit.

topics.title: 1.6 Alternate String Combiner
topics.description: Combine two strings by taking characters alternately. Remaining characters are appended at the end.

ispo.desc: Takes two input strings.
Allocates StringBuilder accumulator and index tracking variable in storage memory.
Loops while indices are within boundaries, alternately copying characters into the accumulator.
Converts StringBuilder output storage to String and returns it.

quiz.q: What is the result of combining 'Hello' and 'World' alternately?

topics.title: 1.7 Padovan Sequence
topics.description: A sequence defined by the recurrence relationship: P(n) = P(n-2) + P(n-3) with P(0)=P(1)=P(2)=1.

ispo.desc: Receives count parameter 'num' indicating terms to output.
Allocates p0, p1, and p2 variables to store the previous three values of the sequence.
Allocates temporary variable pNext inside loop to hold computed value.
Loop computes the next term as p0 + p1, and shifts variables to slide the calculation window.
Prints base elements directly to standard output.
Prints each computed sequence element to standard output.

quiz.q: What is the value of P(5) in the Padovan sequence?

topics.title: 1.8 Leaders in an Array
topics.description: An element is a leader if it is greater than all elements on its right. The rightmost element is always a leader.

ispo.desc: Takes array 'arr' and its size 'n' as input parameters.
Instantiates an ArrayList in memory to accumulate discovered leader elements.
Declares and stores currentMax to track the largest element found from the right side scan.
Loops backwards. Checks if current element >= currentMax, updates currentMax and registers leader if true.
Reverses the leaders list to restore the correct left-to-right ordering.
Returns the final list of leader values.

quiz.q: In the array {16, 17, 4, 3, 5, 2}, what are the leaders?

topics.title: 1.9 Number Raised to its Reverse
topics.description: Find the value of number N raised to the power of its reverse digits R, modulo 10^9 + 7.

ispo.desc: Accepts parameters base N and exponent R.
Stores prime modulo value (10^9 + 7) as MOD constant.
Declares storage variables result, base, and exp to execute power modulo logic without modifying parameters.
Implements binary exponentiation loop. Squaring the base and halving exponent in each step.
Returns calculated power modulo result.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of using modulo 10^9 + 7 in exponentiation algorithms?
o: To prevent arithmetic overflow by keeping numbers within standard integer widths
To check if number is prime

topics.title: 1.10 Mean of Array using Recursion
topics.description: Find the average of array elements recursively. Mean(arr, n) = (Mean(arr, n-1) * (n-1) + arr[n-1]) / n.

ispo.desc: Receives array reference 'arr' and number of active elements 'n'.
Declares local variables subMean and currentMean on stack to hold computed step values.
Applies recursive base checks, calculates mean for n-1 subsegment, and extrapolates mean for n elements.
Returns first element directly when base case (n=1) is hit.
Returns overall computed average value.

quiz.q: What is the base case condition for recursively calculating the mean of an array?

topics.title: 1.11 Kth Smallest Element
topics.description: Find the K-th smallest element in a given array of distinct integers.

ispo.desc: Accepts target array and position ranking 'k'.
Declares result variable to store selected element from index k-1.
Sorts the input array in-place.
Returns the element at index k-1.

quiz.q: If array is {7, 10, 4, 3, 20, 15} and k=3, what is the 3rd smallest element?

topics.title: 1.12 Count Pairs with Given Sum
topics.description: Given an array of integers and target K, find the number of pairs whose sum equals K.

complexity.time: O(n^2) nested loops (or O(n) using hashing)

ispo.desc: Accepts array, length, and sum target K.
Declares pairCount to accumulate matches.
Double nested iterations comparing each pair combination and incrementing counter if sum matches K.
Returns counted pairs.

quiz.q: For array {1, 5, 7, 1} and target sum K=6, what is the count of pairs?
